title.rendered: Porta Cabin in Noida: SAMAN’s Factory-Direct Guide to Sizes, Prices & Local Delivery

content.rendered:
Buying a porta cabin in Noida is a different procurement decision than buying one in Delhi, Gurgaon, or Faridabad — and not for the reasons aggregator listings suggest. The Authority you deal with is Noida Authority and YEIDA, the industrial geography is four distinct belts (not Delhi’s compass zones), and the GRAP enforcement timing differs from Delhi NCT. SAMAN’s own factory at Bisrakh Road, Jalpura, Greater Noida sits inside the Noida-Greater Noida belt itself — your cabin is not being “delivered into NCR” but dispatched within the same belt where it was built. Here are the five Noida-specific decisions that actually shape what you pay.

Why a Porta Cabin in Noida Is a Different Procurement Decision from the Rest of NCR

Noida is not one buyer market — it is four. Each belt has its own industries, sector ranges, and demand drivers, and the porta cabin spec that fits one belt rarely fits another.

The Noida core covers Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 — Sectors 1-78 on the residential and commercial side, with the Phase 2 industrial belt running through Sectors 57-65 and Phase 3 stretching into Sectors 80-85. Buyers here are MSME factory owners, sector-level RWA admins, and contractors handling small-to-mid commercial fitouts.

Greater Noida central is a different buyer market entirely. The catchment runs from Pari Chowk through Knowledge Park I-V into Sectors Alpha, Beta, and Gamma. Demand is driven by educational institutions, larger industrial plots, and IT campuses that need site offices for their long-cycle expansion projects.

Greater Noida West — known informally as Noida Extension — is where most of the new residential demand is now landing. Sectors 1, 2, 3, 4, 10, 16, 16B, and 16C are the active belt: emerging group-housing societies and RWA committees buying guard cabins, site admin offices for under-construction projects, and small commercial cabins for retail kiosks.

The YEIDA belt is the newest and the fastest-growing. The Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority sectors — 17, 18, 21, 22 — and the Jewar Noida International Airport zone are driving an entirely separate wave of demand: warehouse site offices, labour bunkhouses for construction crews on data-centre and logistics-park projects, and supervisor cabins for the airport-zone civil works.

When a buyer in any of these four belts searches for a porta cabin in Noida, they need to land on the spec, size, and price band that fits their belt — not a generic Noida listing that lumps a 6×6 guard cabin and a 30×10 multi-room industrial cabin into the same page. The full portable site cabin range SAMAN builds at our Greater Noida factory at Jalpura is engineered around exactly that buyer-belt logic.

What Does a Porta Cabin in Noida Actually Cost? Real INR Bands by Size (May 2026)

A porta cabin in Noida ranges from roughly ₹1.25 lakh for a 6×6 ft mini unit to ₹4.5–5+ lakh for a 30×10 ft multi-room cabin, factory-direct from a verified manufacturer. Aggregator listings on IndiaMart Noida start as low as ₹35,000 but are typically cement-fiber tier — a structurally different product class than an MS-frame cabin.

What the SAMAN factory-direct range looks like by size — and what the same nominal size costs from an IndiaMart Noida supplier:

Size SAMAN factory-direct (Noida dispatch) IndiaMart Noida aggregator range Typical Noida use case
6×6 ft mini ₹1,25,000 – ₹1,65,000 ₹35,000 – ₹66,000 (cement-fiber tier) Sector guard cabin / RWA gate
10×10 ft ₹2,15,000 – ₹2,65,000 ₹1,35,000 – ₹1,65,000 SME admin office / IT-park temporary admin
20×8 ft ₹2,85,000 – ₹3,55,000 ₹1,71,000 – ₹2,09,000 Construction site office / Phase 2 factory plot
20×10 ft ₹3,75,000 – ₹4,25,000 ₹1,89,000 – ₹2,31,000 Multi-section site office / labour bunkhouse split
30×10 ft (multi-room) ₹4,50,000 – ₹5,35,000+ Limited listings, custom-quote IT-park admin block / YEIDA warehouse supervisor + crew cabin

The IndiaMart Noida and Greater Noida directories list cabins starting around ₹35,000–50,000 for a 6×6 ft guard cabin and ₹54,000–66,000 for a 6×6×10 ft cabin. Those numbers are real — but they are almost always cement-fiber-board or PVC-panel cabins on a light iron-pipe frame. That is a different product class from an MS-frame porta cabin built to IS 2062 Grade A mild steel with 50–75 mm PUF sandwich panels at 38–40 kg/m³ density.

The cement-fiber-board cabin works for a short-term security post at a single project. The MS-frame cabin is built to survive 8–10 site relocations, three monsoon seasons on an exposed industrial plot, and the structural load of a 30-person SME office floor. The price difference is not “premium versus budget” — it is “purpose-built versus disposable”. A buyer comparing our Noida factory-direct price sheet to a ₹50,000 IndiaMart listing is comparing two unrelated products. The right question is which product class fits the project, not which one is cheaper in headline numbers.

Two practical implications follow from this. First, when SAMAN quotes ₹2,15,000 for a 10×10 ft cabin, that price includes the IS 2062 Grade A frame, the panel-density-certified PUF panels, the 5-year structural warranty, and the GST — not a stripped-down headline. Second, a buyer who has been quoted ₹1.4 lakh for a 10×10 ft cabin on IndiaMart Noida should ask the supplier three questions before paying: what gauge is the frame steel, what is the panel density in kg/m³, and is the warranty 5 years on the structure or 6 months on everything? The answers will tell you which tier you are buying — and which tier you are actually paying for.

Delivery from Our Greater Noida Unit at Jalpura — Real Lead Times by Noida Sector Group

Our factory at Khata No 226, Vill-Jalpura, Bisrakh Road, Dadri, Greater Noida 201308 is roughly 12 km from Pari Chowk, 18–25 km from most of the Greater Noida West sectors, 30–40 km from the Noida Authority core, and 45–60 km from the YEIDA / Jewar airport belt depending on the access route. Lead times below assume a finished cabin in stock or a standard build complete — bespoke layouts add 7–14 days to the dispatch window.

Noida core (Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3 — Sectors 1–78 plus industrial Sectors 57–65, 80–85, 100–150): typical dispatch 1–2 days, accessed via the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway, with entry through Sector 142 / Sector 132 / Sector 18 depending on destination. Heavy-vehicle entry is generally permitted off-peak; project sites with internal Authority restrictions add half a day for the gate-clearance window.

Greater Noida core (Pari Chowk, Knowledge Park I–V, Sectors Alpha, Beta, Gamma): same-day dispatch is possible if the cabin is in stock and the buyer can take delivery on a working weekday. Most deliveries land within 24 hours of factory clearance.

Greater Noida West / Noida Extension (Sectors 1, 2, 3, 4, 10, 16, 16B, 16C): typical dispatch 1–2 days. Access via the GN West Link Road or via the NH-24 → Greater Noida Expressway interchange. Group-housing societies with single-gate entry need advance dispatch coordination; we publish the standard truck dimensions in our delivery confirmation so the RWA can pre-clear the gate.

YEIDA plus Yamuna Expressway industrial belt plus Jewar Noida International Airport zone (Sectors 17, 18, 21, 22 plus airport civil-works area): typical dispatch 2–3 days. Route is Bisrakh-Jalpura via Pari Chowk to the Yamuna Expressway entry at Sector Pi or Sector Omega, then south. Toll passage and access permits for the airport civil-works zone are typically project-side responsibility — we coordinate the dispatch slot to the project’s gate-pass window.

The GRAP factor matters between November and February. When the Commission for Air Quality Management invokes Stage III, heavy-vehicle movement inside Noida-Greater Noida tightens — non-CNG and non-BS-VI heavy goods vehicles face restrictions until the air-quality index drops below the trigger band. We re-schedule dispatch to the Stage-II windows that typically open in the early hours and after the evening peak. Tell us your installation deadline up front during GRAP months; we plan the dispatch around the window, not against it.

Noida Authority Compliance, RWA Permissions, and the Documents You Should Ask For

A porta cabin is classified by Noida Authority and Greater Noida Authority as a temporary movable structure — not a permanent building under the Authority’s building bye-laws. That classification matters: it determines whether you need an Authority NOC and what documentation you carry on-site if inspection happens.

For a porta cabin installed on a private industrial plot or a project site inside the Authority’s allotted sectors, no separate NOC is typically required from the Authority itself — the plot’s existing approval covers temporary site-office structures. What is asked for, almost always, is the manufacturer’s documentation pack: GSTIN of the manufacturer, factory address proof, mill test certificate for the structural steel, panel-density certification, and the written warranty terms.

For a porta cabin installed on a commercial plot inside a Sector 18 / Sector 38A / Sector 104–126 commercial belt, the same rule applies — but the building society or commercial complex’s facility office usually asks for the same five documents plus a structural-load declaration if the cabin sits on an elevated plinth or has crowd-occupancy use (retail kiosk, ticket counter, public-facing facility).

For an RWA or group-housing society installation — guard cabin at the society gate, admin cabin for the society office, builder-handover site office during construction — the RWA committee or the developer’s site project office is the approving party, not the Authority. The committee asks for the same documentation pack plus an undertaking that the cabin will be removed or relocated when the underlying use ends.

For an installation inside the YEIDA / Jewar airport civil-works zone, the project’s principal contractor manages the cabin’s permitting under their overall site-works approval. As the porta cabin manufacturer in Noida supplying the unit, SAMAN provides the cabin’s documentation; the contractor handles the gate-pass and Authority interface.

The five-document procurement pack SAMAN provides on every Noida delivery: GSTIN-verifiable invoice, factory-of-origin proof at our Jalpura, Bisrakh Road unit, mill test certificate confirming IS 2062 Grade A mild steel for the structural frame, PUF panel density certification at 38–40 kg/m³, and a written warranty letter stating the 5-year structural and 1–2-year finishing-fitting terms. For an RWA committee or an SME procurement officer signing off on a higher-spec layout — our multi-room cabin with finished interiors routinely passes society-level scrutiny because the documentation chain is complete from the day of dispatch.

Choosing a Porta Cabin for Your Specific Noida Use Case

The right cabin spec is not “MS-frame, the biggest you can afford” — it is the spec that matches your buyer profile, your Noida belt, and your project’s actual time horizon. Four common buyer profiles cover most Noida demand.

The construction-site contractor working a Sector 142–150 commercial expansion or a Greater Noida West group-housing project typically needs a 20×8 ft or 20×10 ft MS-frame site office porta cabin. The site engineer, the project manager, and the documentation officer share the cabin during the day; the watchman’s role is usually separate. SAMAN’s 20×8 ft and 20×10 ft size points include an electrical-fittings pack, a wash-area provision if needed, and a roof rated for monsoon load — typical dispatch 1–2 days from Jalpura.

The SME or industrial-unit owner with a Phase 2 plot in Sectors 57–65 or a Phase 3 plot in Sectors 80–85 usually wants a 10×10 ft or 20×8 ft admin office on the plot — separate from the production floor for visitors, accounts, and procurement meetings. The decision often turns on whether the unit also wants a small attached pantry or toilet partition. The 10×10 ft format covers single-room admin needs comfortably; the 20×8 ft is the right call when a partition for a small meeting space or a wash area is needed. For buyers benchmarking against per-sq-ft rates, our national porta cabin cost benchmark publishes the rate ranges by size band with a worked total-project example.

The RWA committee, society admin, or builder-handover team in the Greater Noida West Sector 1–16, 16B, 16C residential belt or in the Sector 50–78 Noida group-housing pockets typically buys two cabin formats: a 6×6 ft or 8×8 ft guard cabin for the society gate, and a 10×10 ft admin or committee cabin for the society’s site management during construction or initial handover. The guard cabin is the higher-volume order; we have shipped these units into Greater Noida West RWA committees in steady volume through the last 18 months.

The warehouse, logistics, YEIDA, or Jewar airport-zone buyer in Sectors 17, 18, 21, 22 along the Yamuna Expressway or in the Jewar airport civil-works belt usually needs a 20×10 ft multi-room cabin (driver bunkhouse split plus supervisor cabin) or a 30×10 ft multi-room cabin for larger crews. These deployments are typically 12–24 months in duration — long enough that the buy decision beats rental on most calculations, and short enough that a relocatable cabin holds residual value at project end.

Frequently Asked Questions About Porta Cabin in Noida

1. What is the price of a porta cabin in Noida?
A porta cabin in Noida ranges from roughly ₹1.25 lakh for a 6×6 ft mini unit to ₹4.5–5+ lakh for a 30×10 ft multi-room cabin, factory-direct from a verified MS-frame manufacturer. IndiaMart Noida listings start as low as ₹35,000 but are typically cement-fiber tier — a structurally different product class. The right price depends on which product class fits your project, not on the headline number.

2. How long does delivery to Noida take from your Greater Noida factory?
Dispatch from our Jalpura, Bisrakh Road unit is typically same-day to 1 day for the Greater Noida core (Pari Chowk, Knowledge Park) and 1–2 days for the Noida core (Phase 1, 2, 3) and Greater Noida West (Sectors 1–16). The YEIDA / Jewar airport belt typically takes 2–3 days. During GRAP Stage III windows (Nov–Feb), we re-schedule heavy-vehicle dispatch around the air-quality restriction timing.

3. Do I need Noida Authority NOC to install a porta cabin on my plot?
For a porta cabin on a private industrial plot, commercial plot, or project site already approved by Noida Authority or Greater Noida Authority, no separate NOC is typically required — the cabin is classified as a temporary movable structure and the plot’s existing approval covers it. What you need to carry on-site is the manufacturer’s documentation pack: GSTIN, mill test certificate, panel-density certification, and warranty letter. For RWA installations, the society committee — not the Authority — is the approving party.

4. What is the difference between an MS-frame porta cabin and a cement-fiber-board cabin?
The structural frame, the panel system, and the lifespan. An MS-frame porta cabin uses IS 2062 Grade A mild steel for the load-bearing frame and 50–75 mm PUF panels at 38–40 kg/m³ density. A cement-fiber-board cabin uses light iron pipe plus cement-fiber-board panel — cheaper to build, lighter to transport, but rated for fewer site relocations and shorter service life. The two are different product classes, not budget-versus-premium versions of the same product.

5. Is buying or renting a porta cabin cheaper for a Noida project under 18 months?
Below 12 months, rental is usually cheaper. Above 18–22 months, buying tends to be cheaper because the purchase recovers cumulative rent and the cabin retains residual value at project end (if it is MS-frame and well-maintained). Between 12 and 18 months, the math depends on the size, the cabin spec, and whether the project might extend. Our national porta cabin cost benchmark publishes a worked break-even calculation.

6. How do I verify that a porta cabin manufacturer in Noida is genuine, not a reseller?
Ask for five things before you pay: a GSTIN you can verify on the GST portal, a verifiable factory address (not just a sales office), the mill test certificate for the structural steel, the PUF panel density specification sheet, and a written warranty letter naming the manufacturer (not a dealer name). A genuine manufacturer publishes all five without resistance. A reseller will deflect on at least two.

Get a Quote for Your Noida Project

For Noida and Greater Noida buyers, the fastest route is the Delhi NCR line — calls and WhatsApp messages typically return a quote within 4–6 working hours, and our factory at Jalpura, Bisrakh Road, Dadri can dispatch to most Noida belts within 1–3 days from order confirmation.

Delhi NCR / Noida belt — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937

Bangalore (head office) — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859

Tell us four things in your first message — the cabin size you need, the Noida sector or pin code, the project duration, and whether you need any non-standard add-ons (toilet partition, AC pre-wiring, multi-room layout). That is enough for us to confirm a price band, a dispatch window, and the documentation pack in our first reply.

excerpt.rendered: Discover everything about porta cabins – features, benefits, types, and pricing in India. Find the perfect portable cabin solution for your business or personal needs with SAMAN.

author.name: Aman Dubey
author.description: Aman Dubey is the lead marketing and copywriting expert at Saman Portable, specializing in prefabricated, shipping-container, and modular structures. With over 15 years of industry experience, Aman has helped global clients optimize design, streamline production, and amplify brand visibility through data-driven content and strategic SEO. Under his guidance, Saman Portable has become a trusted name in container office solutions and turnkey prefabricated buildings across India and beyond.

caption.rendered: A 20×10 ft MS-frame porta cabin from SAMAN’s Greater Noida factory deployed as a site office on an active sector plot.
